summary: Recent updates to WebRender, the GPU compositor used in Firefox for rendering graphics, have introduced significant enhancements aimed at performance and visual fidelity. One key change is the addition of **corner-shape support for CSS**, which allows more precise design of elements with rounded shapes without needing complex workarounds. Additionally, several legacy image processing paths have been removed, promoting a cleaner codebase for handling images, and support for new YUV formats has been added, improving video rendering quality. These changes matter because they not only streamline the rendering pipeline and enhance visual effects but also prepare the system for future advancements related to content processing and graphical capabilities.
